Title: Use of plankton-derived vitamin B1 precursors, especially thiazole-related precursor, by key marine picoeukaryotic phytoplankton

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for metabolism and growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to condense HET-P-derived thiazole intermediate with HMP-PP to form TMP in the updated pathway model, but not directly tested in this study
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway inference from genome content and updated metabolic model; no direct perturbation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Th1 is included in the updated Ostreococcus B1 salvage model as the enzyme forming TMP from HET-P and HMP-PP. 2. Its role is inferred from pathway annotation, not experimentally validated here.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing minimal thiamine salvage pathways in marine phytoplankton
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in pathway model and discussion; not a primary experimental subject in Results

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Generates active thiamine diphosphate cofactor required for metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to convert thiamine/TMP-derived intermediate to TDP in Ostreococcus B1 pathway; not directly tested
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway inference from genome content and updated metabolic model
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Thi80 is part of the updated Ostreococcus B1 biosynthesis/salvage scheme leading to TDP production. 2. Its contribution is inferred, not experimentally tested in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for pathway reconstruction studies of thiamine metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background pathway component in Figure 8 rather than a direct experimental focus

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: HET or an unidentified thiazole-related precursor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: HET-P or a phosphorylated thiazole-related intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates 4-methyl-5-thiazoleethanol (HET) in canonical systems; in this study is required to utilize an unidentified thiazole-related precursor for thiamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies thiazole-phosphate intermediate required for TDP synthesis from precursors
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream salvage step upstream of TMP/TDP formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step for precursor utilization; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor in central metabolism supporting phytoplankton growth and survival
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for growth of O. tauri RCC745 on HMP plus bacterially supplied thiazole-related precursor(s); ΔThiM mutant cannot use precursors and grows only with intact B1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion (ΔThiM); co-culture growth assays with Marinobacter sp. OTB1; bioassays comparing wild type and ΔThiM; B1 and HMP supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ThiM is essential for O. tauri RCC745 to salvage vitamin B1 from HMP plus a bacterially supplied thiazole-related precursor. 2. Deletion of thiM abolished growth stimulation by HMP in co-culture with Marinobacter sp. OTB1. 3. The ΔThiM mutant retained growth only when intact B1 was supplied, showing ThiM acts specifically in precursor-based thiamine salvage.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus in wild type; knockout generated by homologous recombination using pH4:KanMx:Tef selection cassette
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics/phylogenetic sequence analysis of ThiM; accession IDs from UniProt/JGI mentioned but not enumerated in text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: B1-deplete medium; HMP addition; co-culture with Marinobacter sp. OTB1; antibiotics to block bacterial growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ΔThiM causes inability to use environmental thiazole-related precursors, potentially reducing fitness in B1-poor waters
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker gene to dissect vitamin B1 precursor salvage pathways in marine algae; not a crop breeding target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ostreococcus and Micromonas spp. possess ThiM but cannot use HET, implying the physiological substrate is an unidentified thiazole-related precursor rather than canonical HET
